answers.answer: B) A delete endpoint using UUID v4 for the :id parameter, returning ISO 8601 timestamps in the response, and using RFC 7807 format for errors (e.g., user not found). Reason: This correctly applies ALL three established conventions from the conversation: (1) UUID v4 for IDs (Message 3); (2) ISO 8601 timestamps in UTC (Message 8); (3) RFC 7807 Problem Details for errors (Message 14). Option A ignores all conventions. Option C gets UUID right but ignores timestamp and error format. Option D asks unnecessarily when conventions were already established.
